channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Trump admin declares Maine violated Title IX by letting trans athletes play in girls' sports
item.description: The Department of Health and Human Services served a notice of Title IX violation to Maine after it defied Trump's executive order to protect girls' sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One-of-a-kind card featuring jersey patch of Pirates star Paul Skenes is heading to auction
item.description: PITTBURGH (AP) — Paul Skenes has been in the major leagues for less than a year. A piece of the Pittsburgh Pirates ace — well, OK, a piece of the jersey that Skenes donned during his first start last May — could be ready to join some elite company.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Special Olympics Illinois athletes head to Italy for snowshoe races
item.description: On Wednesday, a team of Special Olympics Illinois snowshoe athletes boarded a plane to New York, with a final destination of Turin, Italy. There they will compete in the Special Olympics Winter World Games.
item.dc:creator: Erin McElroy, NewsNation

item.title: 49ers free agency: Position-by-position look at defense that could add Joey Bosa
item.description: Free agency is the 49ers' first chance to spruce up their defense, perhaps with Nick Bosa's brother Joey, for Robert Saleh's encore as coordinator.
item.dc:creator: Cam Inman, The Mercury News

item.title: Michigan basketball’s bid for Big Ten title derailed by another lackluster home performance
item.description: The No. 17-ranked Michigan Wolverines men’s basketball team must be studied by archaeologists, as their poor play down the stretch of the season with the chance to secure a Big Ten Conference championship is head-scratching.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Carr, Local 4

item.title: Harden has 24th 50-point game, 1 shy of Kobe Bryant for 3rd all-time, as Obama watches
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — James Harden delivered another 50-point game Wednesday night, putting on a throwback performance in the Clippers' new arena.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Harden scores 50 points for 1st time with Clippers as Los Angeles beats Pistons 123-115
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — James Harden scored 50 points, his 24th career 50-point game and first with the Clippers, and Los Angeles beat the Detroit Pistons 123-115 on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pritchard, White become 1st Boston duo to score 40+ in same game, 1st duo in NBA history with 9+ 3s
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Payton Pritchard and Derrick White have been mostly glue guys during their time with the Boston Celtics, filling in gaps and providing sparks when necessary.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chargers release linebacker Joey Bosa after 9 seasons with the franchise
item.description: The Los Angeles Chargers released Joey Bosa on Wednesday night, ending the linebacker's nine-season tenure with the franchise. The move, which came less than a week before the start of the new league year, was expected due to Bosa's large salary cap number and injury history.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cardinals star Jordan Walker sustains bizarre injury during spring training game
item.description: St. Louis Cardinals outfielder Jordan Walker's day was cut short when he exited a spring training game in the third inning with a knee injury.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boys Basketball: Carlsbad 59, Fairmont Prep 55
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- 2025 CIF State Boys Basketball Championships Division I Carlsbad defeated Fairmont Prep 59-55. The visitors made the trek from Anaheim, and they could not slow down UCSD commit Jake Hall who hit for a game high 32 points for the Lancers.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Chase Briscoe wins appeal over modified spoiler during Daytona 500 pole-winning run
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Chase Briscoe and Joe Gibbs Racing won their appeal Wednesday when the National Motorsports Appeals Panel said his Toyota did not have an illegally modified spoiler when he won the Daytona 500 pole.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats at Trump's speech showed how 'out of touch' they are with Americans, ex-NFL kicker says
item.description: Ex-NFL kicker Jay Feely unloaded on Democrats for how they acted during President Donald Trump's address to a joint session of Congress on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Knight, Coyne Schofield lead mix of experience, youth on US women world hockey championship roster
item.description: The United States is bringing a mix of familiar faces — starting with Hilary Knight and Kendall Coyne Schofield — and youth to the women’s world hockey championship tournament in the Czech Republic next month.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chiefs trading All-Pro offensive guard Joe Thuney to Bears for 2026 4th-round pick, AP source says
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — The Kansas City Chiefs are trading two-time All-Pro guard Joe Thuney to the Chicago Bears for a fourth-round pick in the 2026 draft, a person familiar with the deal told The Associated Press on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gerlinde Kaltenbrunner climbed the world's 14 highest peaks without oxygen or porters
item.description: Gerlinde Kaltenbrunner, an Austrian-born mountaineer, became the first woman to climb the world's 14 highest peaks without the help of oxygen or porters.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Celtics honor Somerset teen who saved choking girl
item.description: Ben Courville, a junior firefighter with the Dighton Fire Department, saved a girl last summer from choking at a Fall River restaurant.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, NewsNation

item.title: Girls Soccer CIFSDS Open Division Championship: San Marcos 2, Bonita Vista 1
item.description: The CIFSDS Open Division Girls Soccer Championship was held at Mission Bay High School between the San Marcos Knights (17-4-3) and the Bonita Vista Barons (12-5-2).
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: San Diego FC makes its home debut before a sellout crowd in a city hungry for top-level soccer
item.description: A match decades in the making finally happens Saturday night when San Diego FC debuts on its home field in front of a sellout crowd of fans starved for elite professional sports — and particularly hungry for top-level soccer.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Avalanche land Lindgren, Wild get Nyquist and Panthers acquire Seth Jones in busy day of NHL trades
item.description: Defensemen Seth Jones and Ryan Lindgren and forward Gustav Nyquist were on the move on Saturday as three Central Division teams got a jump on the NHL’s looming trade deadline.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: RSL shuts out Seattle in MLS home opener, 2-0
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Having scored just one goal in its first three games of the 2025 campaign, Real Salt Lake got some help from the Seattle Sounders on its way to a 2-0 victory in its MLS home opener Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Florida Man Games return for Round 2 as teams vie to be the world’s worst superhero
item.description: The Florida Man Games are back for Round 2, as teams from around the state gather to prove they have what it takes to join the ranks of the world’s worst superhero.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: John Collins leads Jazz to 117-116 win over Minnesota
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Walker Kessler had a go-ahead dunk with 9.7 seconds left to help the Utah Jazz beat the Minnesota Timberwolves 117-116 on Friday night, snapping a six-game losing streak in the series.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
